fix: ruff discovery via _find_ruff() — works when venv not in PATH

TerminalTool spawns /bin/sh which doesn't inherit codator's venv PATH.
_find_ruff() resolves ruff from sys.executable's directory (same venv bin
that codator runs in), falling back to system PATH.

This ensures lint baseline capture and verification work consistently
whether codator runs via 'python -m codator' (venv active) or as a
nohup web server (no venv in PATH).

Fix i18n config returning null in Vite dev SSR (#319)

* Fix i18n config returning null in Vite dev SSR

The admin manifest endpoint was returning i18n: null in Vite dev mode
due to a module singleton mismatch between static and dynamic imports.

In getManifest(), the i18n config was loaded via dynamic import(),
but the middleware that calls setI18nConfig() uses a static import.
In Vite dev SSR, these resolve to different module instances.

The fix reads i18n config directly from the virtual module
(virtual:emdash/config) which contains the serialized config data
populated by the Astro integration at build time.

Fixes #294

* Update packages/core/src/emdash-runtime.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(cache): cover gc is_dir false branch for non-directory entries

Add gc_skips_non_directory_entries_in_entries_dir test to exercise
the false branch of `entry.path().is_dir()` in Cache::gc().

When the entries/ directory contains a plain file (not a subdirectory),
the GC loop short-circuits at the `is_dir()` check and leaves the
file untouched. Previously this branch was never hit because all
existing GC tests only placed directories inside entries/.

Branch coverage: 93.36% → 93.40% (137 → 136 missed branches)

* fix: update E2E nav selectors for module-driven ActivityBar buttons

The §29 Phase 3 migration (8a81e9e) changed nav items from <a> links
to <button> elements, but E2E tests still used getByRole('link').
This caused all navigation-related E2E tests to time out at 31.5s.

Changes:

The OAuth step now shows two Copy buttons (homepage URL + callback URL).
Use .first() to avoid Playwright strict mode violation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Spec updated: §29.5.10 marked as delivered with implementation details.
Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit
- Enable/disable audit
- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.9 settings provenance tracking — show value source in Settings UI (#254)

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields
so users can see which layer a resolved value comes from.

- Add Default property to C# ModuleSettingsFieldSchema for manifest defaults
- Compute provenance client-side from stored values vs schema defaults
- Show effective values including defaults when no stored value exists
- Update spec §29.5.9 with implementation details

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.9 prefetch hints — low-priority module bundle prefetching (#257)

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:
- New: prefetchManager.ts — manages loader registry, queue, budget, cancel
- types.ts: add prefetchModules?: string[] to ModuleManifest + DTO + validation
- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches
  Agents and Sessions
- ModuleContainer.tsx: trigger prefetch when module reaches Ready/Mounted
- moduleRegistry.ts: cancel queued prefetches on disable (both own and hinted)
- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.7 per-module performance monitoring (#259)

Add frontend-only metrics collector that tracks per-module lifecycle
events (init timing, mount count, suspend/resume, faults) and surfaces
them in the Settings → Modules UI.

New files:
- moduleMetrics.ts — singleton collector with timer API, ring buffer,
  onChange subscriptions
- ModulePerformancePanel.tsx — diagnostic UI with metric grid and
  collapsible event log
- moduleMetrics.test.ts — 19 unit tests

Instrumentation:
- ModuleContainer: init timing (wraps onInit), mount tracking with
  StrictMode dedup via compound key ref, fault/dispose events
- moduleRegistry: suspend, resume, dispose, render-crash events

Spec §29.7.7 updated from MAY/planned to implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: mask secret fields in GetProjectSettings response (#256) (#260)

The GetProjectSettings endpoint returned decrypted secret values to the
client. Now checks ModuleSettingsFieldSchema.Secret for each key and
returns '***' for secret fields, consistent with GetAllAsync masking.

Added test: GetProjectSettings_MasksSecretFields

Closes #256

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.8 memory leak detection in dev mode

Track module memory footprint before init and after dispose + GC delay.
Warn in console and Performance panel if retained memory exceeds 50 MB.

- Uses performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory() with legacy fallback
- Versioned snapshots prevent race conditions on rapid remount
- Gated behind import.meta.env.DEV — tree-shaken in production
- 7 new tests (26 total in moduleMetrics suite)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)
- SqliteModuleVisibilityStore with transactional replace (Infrastructure)
- Migration 014: module_visibility table
- GET/PUT /api/module/visibility/project/{projectId} endpoints
- Audit logging for visibility changes
- DI registration + singleton allowlist

Frontend:
- API client functions for visibility endpoints
- moduleStore tracks hiddenByProject per project ID
- ActivityBar filters out hidden modules
- Retry-on-failure: transient fetch errors don't cache false positives

Tests:
- 6 unit tests for SqliteModuleVisibilityStore
- 5 unit tests for ModuleController visibility endpoints
- Architecture singleton allowlist updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/moduleRegistry.ts — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/modules/ModuleContainer.tsx — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests
- docs/spec/29-module-system.md — updated §29.8.5 to reflect impl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update §29 Phase 4 checklist with completed items

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.6 project deletion cleanup (#267)

Implement project-scoped data cleanup when a project is deleted.

Backend:
- Add DeleteForProjectAsync to IModuleSettingsStore, IPluginSettingsStore,
  and IModuleVisibilityStore domain interfaces
- Implement in SQLite stores with LIKE-escaped project ID matching
- New IProjectCleanupService orchestrator calls all three stores
- DELETE /api/module/project/{projectId} endpoint with 207 on partial failure
- Audit logging for cleanup events

Frontend:
- cleanupProjectModuleData() API client function
- projectCache.clearProject() already existed for in-memory cleanup

Safety:
- SQL LIKE wildcards in projectId are escaped to prevent cross-project deletion
- Partial store failures return HTTP 207 with details (not masked as 200 OK)
- Each store cleanup is independent — one failure doesn't block others

The project system does not yet have a project entity with CRUD operations.
This provides the cleanup building blocks ready to wire when project
deletion is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: mark delivered P1 items with ✅ in §29 spec headers

Mark §29.2.6 (Keep-Alive), §29.4.8 (SDK Utility Hooks), §29.6.6
(Navigation Guards), §29.9.2 (Status Bar), §29.9.3 (Command Palette),
and §29.13.5 (Dev Mode) as delivered — all were completed in Phase 2/3
but spec section headers weren't updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.2.9 dispose ordering — reverse nav order on shutdown

Add disposeAll() to ModuleRegistry that disposes modules in reverse
navigation.order (highest first, core modules last). Wired to pagehide
event in App.tsx. Includes idempotency guard (isShutdown) to prevent
double-disposal when React unmount runs after disposeAll(). Respects
BFCache by checking PageTransitionEvent.persisted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.10 bundle integrity — SHA-256 ETag and dev-mode verification

Backend (ModuleController.GetBundle):
- Compute SHA-256 of bundle file contents, return as ETag header
- Handle If-None-Match for 304 Not Modified (proper EntityTagHeaderValue parsing)
- Read file once to guarantee hash/body consistency (bundles capped at 2 MB)

Frontend (modules/bundleIntegrity.ts):
- fetchBundleWithIntegrity() fetches bundle and verifies SHA-256 in dev mode
- Uses SubtleCrypto.digest for client-side hash computation
- Skips verification in production (browser HTTP cache uses ETag natively)
- Re-exported from api/client.ts for consumer convenience

Tests:
- 6 new vitest tests covering: hash match, mismatch rejection, HTTP errors,
  missing ETag, URL encoding, AbortSignal passthrough

Spec: §29.3.10 updated with implementation details, marked ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: lint baseline + explosion detector prevent heal spirals

Root cause: verify() reported pre-existing lint errors (e.g. F401 unused
import) as agent failures, triggering heal cycles that made things worse.

Fixes:
1. _capture_lint_baseline() — snapshots ruff errors on target files BEFORE
   editing. _verify_python_lint() subtracts these so agent is only blamed
   for errors it introduced.

2. _HEAL_SYSTEM prompt — added rule: 'Fixing lint errors you did not
   introduce (F401 unused import etc.) → FORBIDDEN'

3. No-progress detector — now also catches error explosions (3x+ growth),
   not just non-decreasing counts. Prevents 1→21 error spirals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: GPU backward on gfx1010 via SafeIndex autograd workaround

Root cause: PyTorch 2.9.1 ROCm autograd backward kernel for x[idx]
dispatches an invalid HIP kernel when len(idx) > 1024 on gfx1010.
Individual ops (index_add_, scatter_add_) work fine — only the
autograd-generated backward kernel is broken.

Fix: custom _SafeIndex autograd function that uses manual index_add_
for the backward pass. Applied to all tensor indexing in GATLayer.
GPU probe updated to test at realistic sizes (n_nodes=500, E=2000).

Verified: full FaultGAT forward+backward works on GPU for CWRU graph
sizes (444-523 nodes, 1800-2600 edges after reverse edge augmentation).

fix: disable Stylix nvf target and silence GTK4 warning

Two HM evaluation errors fixed:

home/features/theming.nix:
- Add stylix.targets.nvf.enable = false
  Stylix has a dedicated NVF target (modules/neovim/nvf.nix) separate
  from stylix.targets.neovim. With autoEnable = true it was setting
  programs.nvf.settings.vim.theme.name = 'base16', which conflicted
  with the 'gruvbox' theme in home/features/nvim/settings.nix.
  All three neovim-related targets (neovim, vim, nvf) are now
  explicitly disabled so NVF manages its own theme.

- Add gtk.gtk4.theme = null
  HM 26.05 changed the gtk.gtk4.theme default from config.gtk.theme
  to null. Setting it explicitly adopts the new default and silences
  the evaluation warning.

- Add { config, ... }: function signature (required for gtk.gtk4.theme)
- Expand target inventory comment to document gtk + the three NVF targets

commit.message:
fix(solvers): add CPU fallback for OOM during linalg.solve

When the 30k×30k normal-equation solve exceeds GPU memory,
transparently fall back to CPU for the factorization and move
the result back to the original device.  Applied to all three
solver paths: tikhonov, normal_equations (Cholesky), and
solve_from_normal_equations, plus StreamingNEFClassifier.solve().

The solve is a one-shot operation so the CPU overhead is minimal
compared to re-running the entire benchmark.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
